title: A sensitive, selective and extractive spectrophotometric determination of tungsten( VI) using 3-hydroxy-2-phenyl-4-oxo-4H -1-benzopyran
author: Joginder Rohilla
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Kurukshetra University, Kurukshetra-J 36 119, Haryana, India 

 E-mail: jogi001s@ gmail.com; jogi001@reditfmail.com 

  Manuscript received 24 July 2007, accepted 30 July 2007  

 A simple, rapid, sensitive and selective spectrophotometric method for the determination of tungsten(VI) in trace amounts is developed using 3-hydroxy-2-phenyl-4-oxo-4 H -1-benzopyran (HPB) as a reagent for the complexation of metal ion and extracting the 1 : 2 (metal : ligand) complex into dichloromethane from 1 M  HCI solution. It obeys Beer's law in the range 0-2.7µg W VI.  ml -1  with molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity at 391 nm as 4.29 x 10 4  dm 3  mol -1  cm -1  and 0.00434µg W cm -1 , respectively. The method is free from the interference of a large number of elements (39).
